utility_evaluation.REASONING: Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. In the final plan, present items are: health_coverage, professional_liability (not in required format), equipment_coverage (not in required format), travel_coverage, total_budget, special_notes (not in required format). Now check completeness for required ones: health_coverage is present with costs but lacks explicit mention of both physical and mental health; still it is the correct category and has costs. property_coverage is missing (equipment_coverage is not clearly home/contents). travel_coverage is present with costs. additional_services is missing. total_budget is present with monthly/annual totals. Thus, included items from the required list: health_coverage (partially specified regarding mental health, but the format only asks for 'physical and mental health insurance with costs'—the plan name and costs are given, but no explicit confirmation of mental health; since the requirement is specific, it is not fully specified), travel_coverage (has costs), total_budget (has breakdown). Counting only fully specified per the requirement: health_coverage is not fully specified because it doesn't state physical and mental; travel_coverage is fully specified with costs; total_budget has the breakdown. So 2 fully specified items (travel_coverage, total_budget) out of 5.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists four plan names: SecureLife Basic Plan (health_coverage), InsurePlus Standard Plan (professional_liability), Reduced Tier Photography Protection (equipment_coverage), and Minimal EliteTravel Coverage (travel_coverage). We must map only those that appear in the ground-truth ratings. From the provided ground-truth, SecureLife Basic Plan exists under primary_health_coverage.physical_health with rating 4, and InsurePlus Standard Plan exists under multiple categories but relevantly is rated 10 for travel_insurance and 10 for primary physical health. However, in the final plan it's used as professional liability; there is no professional liability section in the ground-truth, so no rating can be mapped for that usage. Reduced Tier Photography Protection and Minimal EliteTravel Coverage do not appear anywhere in the ground-truth, so they cannot be mapped. Therefore, only SecureLife Basic Plan can be mapped to its rating of 4 from the physical health section. If we also consider InsurePlus Standard Plan generically, we must not infer across categories; since professional liability is not rated, we exclude it.
